channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Zipline snaps up another $200M to fuel its drone delivery expansion
item.description: The funding adds to a previous round, announced in January, that valued the drone startup at $7.6 billion.

item.title: ‘Players’ of an MMORPG for AI Agents Spontaneously Generated Their Own Religion
item.description: SpaceMolt is a new destination for vaguely portentous word salad.

item.title: Marvel Studios Is Bringing ‘Wonder Man’ Back for Season 2
item.description: Yahya Abdul-Mateen II and Ben Kingsley will return for the critically acclaimed show.

item.title: Apple sets WWDC for June 8 — and this may be its last best chance to fix Siri and deliver the AI we were promised
item.description: Apple set the week of June 8 for its World Wide Developers Conference, and we're expecting big things in AI, as well as significant updates to every major Apple platform.

item.title: The amazing Kindle Colorsoft just dropped to its record-low Black Friday price on Amazon
item.description: The Kindle Colorsoft is back at its lowest price ever – on sale at Amazon

item.title: Kalshi says it will block politicians and athletes from trading in markets they’re tied to
item.description: The prediction market platform Kalshi says it's rolling out new guardrails to "preemptively block" political candidates and athletes from trading in their own markets, as reported earlier by Axios. In addition to blocking political candidates from trading on their own campaigns, Kalshi says its system will also prevent professional and college sports athletes, referees, and […]...

item.title: Bernie Sanders’ AI ‘gotcha’ video flops, but the memes are great
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders thinks he's tricked Claude into revealing the AI industry's secrets, but he really just exposed how agreeable chatbots can become.

item.title: Why ‘Ready or Not 2’ Had to Include That Shocking Slay
item.description: Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett talk about a major spoiler for their gory horror sequel.

item.title: "This framework can succeed only if it is applied uniformly across the United States": White House rolls out national legislative AI framework that looks to trump state level rules
item.description: Donald Trump wants to take ownership of some AI legislation, agreeing to keep states in control of fraud and consumer protection.

item.title: Google’s new Pixel 10 ads made me go ‘wait, WHAT are they trying to sell?’
item.description: Ever watch a TV ad and wonder "How did this get approved?" Today, Google has not one but two new ad spots for its six-month-old Pixel 10 phones, and… let's just say they may not come across as intended. First, there's "With 100x Zoom," an ad that appears to suggest that if a vacation rental […]

item.title: A Billionaire-Backed Startup Wants to Grow 'Organ Sacks' to Replace Animal Testing
item.description: R3 Bio has a bold idea for replacing lab animals: genetically-engineered whole organ systems that lack a brain. The long-term goal, says a cofounder, is to make human versions.

item.title: Hundreds of NYU instructors are on strike—even after spending spring break at the negotiating table
item.description: Members of NYU’s faculty represented by Contract Faculty United-United Auto Workers are on the picket line after not reaching a contract agreement with the university. Several hundred non-tenured full-time faculty members at New York University are on strike after the school failed to reach a tentative contract agreement with Contract Faculty United-United Auto Workers....

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says ‘I think we’ve achieved AGI’
item.description: On a Monday episode of the Lex Fridman podcast, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang made a hot-button statement: "I think we've achieved AGI." AGI, or artificial general intelligence, is a vaguely defined term that has incited a lot of discussion by tech CEOs, tech workers, and the general public in recent years, as it typically denotes […]

item.title: EA is nuking Battlefield Hardline on consoles
item.description: EA has put another game on the chopping block, or at least the console versions of it. The company says it will delist the PS4 and Xbox One versions of Battlefield Hardline from digital storefronts on May 22, and shut down the online services on June 22. The single-player campaign will remain playable for those who own the game. The PC version of Battlefield Hardline isn’t affected by these changes.In its announcement on X, EA didn't explain exactly why it's ceasing support for the game on PS4 and Xbox One. It pointed readers to a FAQ on its website that lays out some of the typical reasons wh...

item.title: Two of my favorite color e-book readers are the cheapest they’ve been in months
item.description: Color isn’t essential in an e-reader, but let’s be honest, it’s a nice perk that can bring digital books, magazines, comics, cookbooks, and other publications to life. The catch is that color ebook readers tend to be substantially pricier, which makes today’s deals stand out. Right now, the Kindle Colorsoft (16GB) and Kobo Libra Colour […]

item.title: President Trump wants his face on a coin. Maybe he shouldn’t
item.description: The last time a living president was on a coin it didn’t work out so well. On March 19, the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts voted to approve a commemorative gold coin that flies in the face of the law. On one side of the coin President Donald Trump is seen scowling as he leans with his fist on a desk; on the other, a bald eagle carries the Liberty Bell....

item.title: Fin de l’anniversaire AliExpress : voici les dernières meilleures offres sur les produits tech du moment [Sponso]
item.description: Ce 13 mars 2026 signe le début de la période anniversaire du géant de l'e-commerce AliExpress. Pendant neuf jours, les prix des meilleurs produits tech baissent considérablement grâce à divers codes promo. Voici les meilleures offres.

item.title: Russian authorities block paywall removal site Archive.today
item.description: A notice on the popular paywall-bypass website Archive.today said that access is blocked "by decision of [Russian] public authorities."

item.title: Sunken Soviet Submarine Is Quietly Leaking Radiation Decades Later, Study Finds
item.description: While the nuclear-powered attack sub’s reactor appears to be ‘corroded,’ years of collaboration with Russian authorities has avoided a true marine disaster.

item.title: No, for Real, the Best Sci-fi Movie of the Year So Far Is Finally Coming Home
item.description: 'Nirvanna The Band The Show The Movie' comes to digital March 24 and Blu-ray May 26.

item.title: Signal is being targeted by Russian hackers in a huge new phishing campaign, FBI says
item.description: The campaign is allegedly already a success, as Russians access people's devices.

item.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP price today: Crypto market rises on latest Trump move—how long will it last?
item.description: Digital asset prices are getting a much-needed boost Monday. Here’s what investors need to know. Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP, along with the S&P 500, rose on Monday after President Donald Trump said the U.S. will pause strikes on Iran’s power plants and energy infrastructure for five days. (The S&P was up 1.5% in Monday afternoon trading.)

item.title: A Mysterious Numbers Station Is Broadcasting Through the Iran War
item.description: First heard as US and Israeli strikes on Iran began, the shortwave broadcast has since been traced to a US military base in Germany—but its purpose and its operator remain unclear.

item.title: Apple could put ads in Maps as soon as this summer
item.description: Apple will soon bring advertisements to its Maps app on mobile and the web, according to a report from Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. The ads, which Apple could reportedly announce this month before rolling them out in the summer, could allow businesses to pay for the highest spot in search results on Apple Maps. That means […]

item.title: Flipper Zero, Everyone’s Favorite Legally Dubious Hacker Tool, Gets an AI Upgrade
item.description: Old-school Flipper fans don't appear to be thrilled about the project.

item.title: Amazon Big Spring Sale: How long does it last and when does it end?
item.description: Amazon's Big Spring Sale is coming. But how long does the sale last and when does it end? Here's everything you need to know.
